Separately, several Curaçao Gaming Control Board forum threads (2023) contain user complaints alleging Rabidi N.V. casinos, including Fezbet, removed license validator seals and were unresponsive about licensing — flagged for awareness, not used to alter bonus data, but worth noting for overall trust/confidence framing.

The 3-deposit welcome package's existence, wagering multiplier (40x), and general structure are well-supported across the operator's own site and many independent reviews, but exact per-tier amounts vary significantly between the operator's own page and affiliate-exclusive variants — flagged rather than averaged. IE and CA (excl. Ontario) availability is well-confirmed. ZA availability is confirmed via the operator's own homepage. FI availability is plausible (Finnish players are legally free to use offshore Curaçao-licensed sites) but has less direct confirmation and one data point suggesting at least one specific affiliate code excluded FI. Curaçao portfolio as 50 Crowns and 40+ other sister brands (Spinrise, LuckyElf, Boho Casino, Wild Coins, North Casino, Casino Rocket, etc.) — none of that sister-brand data was used here. Headline welcome-package totals vary enormously across affiliate pages (€2,000 official vs. up to €6,500 on an Ireland-exclusive deal vs. up to C$9,750 on a Canada-exclusive deal) — these are almost certainly inflated affiliate-only variants layered on top of the same base 3-deposit structure; the operator's own page was used as the anchor figure.

(bankrupt, Curaçao license revoked in 2023); current operator variously reported as Liernin Enterprises Ltd (per FinTelegram, holding only a Philippines/PAGCOR offshore license not valid in Europe), Araxio Development N.V., or the Casolinia Group, depending on the source
_brand_disambiguation_notes: This casino's ownership and licensing history is unusually unresolved compared to typical Curaçao-family operators. Zet Casino launched in 2018 under Rabidi N.V. with a Curaçao license. Rabidi N.V. went bankrupt in 2023 and that license was revoked. A specialized gambling-compliance investigative outlet (FinTelegram, July 2024) subsequently reported that zetcasino.com is operated by Liernin Enterprises Ltd using only a Philippines/PAGCOR offshore license that does not entitle the site to operate in Europe or other jurisdictions where it is nonetheless accepting players — and flagged it as a compliance risk. Separately, other review sources describe the operator as 'Araxio Development N.V.' (Curaçao) or as part of the 'Casolinia Group' under an Anjouan Gaming Board license, and at least one source implausibly cites an 'Estonian Tax and Customs Board' license, which does not appear to be a real gambling regulator and was disregarded as unreliable. No single authoritative, current license could be confirmed. This is flagged here rather than resolved by picking one story, since it is materially relevant to how much trust should be placed in this operator's other claims (including country availability and bonus terms below).

features: Crypto-first casino with 20+ cryptocurrencies supported

Tiered VIP/rakeback program

_research_confidence: MEDIUM-LOW overall, and unusual in structure compared to typical casino welcome-bonus research. Stake.com's own site does not currently advertise a standard sign-up deposit-match welcome bonus — its featured promotions are wagering-based raffles/races plus a VIP/rakeback program, all confirmed directly on stake.com. A widely-repeated '200% deposit match' welcome offer exists across many affiliate sites, but with inconsistent caps, codes, and wagering terms between them and no confirmation on the operator's own site, so it was flagged LOW confidence rather than presented as fact. Country coverage: IE, FI, and CA (excluding Ontario) were confirmed available via multiple consistent sources. US was excluded — Stake.com is not accessible there; only the separate Stake.us sweepstakes product is, and that was intentionally out of scope for this stake.com research. BR and ZA were NOT included despite being on the target list: four independent sources (each appearing to quote Stake's own Terms of Service restricted-country list) consistently name both Brazil and South Africa as currently restricted/blocked on Stake.com, while two other sources claimed Brazil is supported via a local licence — given this direct conflict and the weight/consistency of the sources indicating restriction, these were omitted per the 'do not guess' rule rather than included. This should be treated as a flag for manual re-verification against Stake's live Terms of Service, not a final answer. Germany intentionally omitted per project scope (and is, in any case, also named as restricted by several sources).
_brand_disambiguation_notes: Two important disambiguations: (1) Stake.com is a distinct product from Stake.us. Stake.us is a US-only sweepstakes-model site (Gold Coins / Stake Cash, no direct real-money wagering) operated for the US market, where Stake.com itself is not accessible. Any 'Stake' bonus content referencing US states, Stake Cash, Gold Coins, or a 'NODEP' no-deposit code belongs to Stake.us and was excluded from this research, which covers stake.com only. (2) One low-quality source (thefaithfulmufc.com) linked to 'casinostakee.eu' as if it were Stake's Irish site — this is a different, unrelated domain and was not used as a source. Separately, the operator's registered name (Medium Rare N.V.) and current Curaçao licence number (OGL/2024/1451/0918) were confirmed directly on stake.com's own footer; older secondary sources citing licence '8048/JAZ' likely reflect a prior licence number before Curaçao's licensing regime consolidation and were not used where they conflicted with the live site.

Two compounding issues: (1) operator/license identity has shifted across multiple entities and jurisdictions over the casino's history, which is unusual even by Curaçao/Anjouan-tier standards and worth independently reconfirming before publishing anything about this casino's legitimacy; (2) bonus mechanics (wagering multiplier, expiry window, free spins count, max cashout) conflict significantly even across sources dated within the same few months of 2026, suggesting either frequent unannounced changes or generally unreliable affiliate coverage of this operator. Country availability for IE, FI, BR, and ZA is reasonably well-supported by an explicit allowed-country list plus dedicated regional reviews. US and UK are clearly restricted. Canada (CA/CA-ON) is contradictory across sources and has been omitted entirely rather than guessed — a crypto-only casino under a non-AGCO licence is very unlikely to be legitimately available in Ontario regardless. Given the self-exclusion complaint and lack of standard responsible-gambling tools, this may also be one worth a broader legitimacy conversation before publishing on IrishLucky rather than just a bonus-data pass.

This pattern of entity/license churn is itself worth flagging as a trust signal, independent of any specific bonus figures — recommend confirming current operator/license directly on the live site's footer before publishing. Separately, a Casino Guru dispute record shows a rejected player complaint alleging Crashino failed to honor a self-exclusion request; independent reviews also note the casino offers no deposit/session limit tools, only self-exclusion — relevant given GRAI's responsible-gambling expectations.

_brand_disambiguation_notes: Hugo Casino (launched 2022-2023) is confirmed as a genuine GBL Solutions N.V. brand, part of a larger Curaçao-licensed casino group whose other sites include Rolling Slots, Need For Spin, Winshark, Ritzo, Spinline, Ludios, Mino Casino, Vegazone, Slota, and Wild Tokyo — all sharing very similar bonus templates, so cross-brand consistency in structure is expected, not a sign of confusion. A significant issue found during research: several affiliate sites advertise dramatically inflated 'exclusive' headline welcome figures for Hugo Casino (€3,450, €5,450, even €5,750), but closer reading of those same sources' detailed breakdowns (and one reviewer's own account of what was actually credited after depositing) shows the real, consistently-corroborated welcome package is far smaller: 225% up to €600 + 275 free spins across three deposits. The inflated headline figures appear to describe the deposit CAP required to unlock a separate, much bigger 'High Roller/VIP' welcome offer (e.g. 'deposit €5,750 to get 50% up to €5,750') rather than what a normal player receives — several low-quality/promotional sources conflate the two. The standard package is used below with HIGH-MEDIUM confidence; the High Roller offer is included but heavily flagged as unreliable given wildly conflicting minimum-deposit/cap figures across sources ($500 to $5,750).

_brand_disambiguation_notes: Bizzo Casino is a genuine sister brand to 20Bet, 22Bet, and National Casino, all operated by TechSolutions Group N.V./TechSolutions (CY) Group Limited under the same Curaçao licence number (8048/JAZ2017-067) seen in the 20Bet research. Despite sharing an operator and platform, Bizzo and 20Bet have genuinely different market access in at least one confirmed case (Italy: 20Bet accepts Italian players, Bizzo restricts them) — this is a useful reminder not to assume identical country coverage across sister brands even under the same licence, so this research was done independently rather than copied from the 20Bet findings. Also worth flagging: some sources reference alternate/mirror domains — bizzoocasino.com (double 'o', used by casinosanalyzer's URL) and bizzooz.com (explicitly described by one source as a mirror the operator shifts to in order to dodge ISP blocks in some regions) — these were not used as primary sources; all data below is attributed to the bizzocasino.com brand as commonly described by mainstream reviewers, though bizzocasino.com itself rendered as an empty JavaScript shell when fetched directly, so nothing here comes from the operator's own page.

The genuine, actively-reviewed Granawin Casino operates at granawin.com (explicitly named by gamblescope.com), run by TechSolutions Group N.V. — the same operator group behind Bizzo Casino, 20Bet, and National Casino (already researched in this series) — under Curaçao GCB certificate OGL/2024/590/0758 and a Kahnawake licence, launched in 2024-2025. Reassuringly, the welcome bonus figures displayed on granawin.online (100% up to €120 + 120 Free Spins, then 50% up to €100 + 50 Free Spins on Wolf Gold; separate sports welcome of 100% up to €100) match what independent, non-affiliated reviewers report for the real granawin.com casino almost exactly, suggesting granawin.online is an affiliate marketing page for the genuine operator rather than an unrelated or fraudulent site — but this could not be fully confirmed, and none of the data below was sourced from granawin.online itself. Recommend confirming with whoever supplied this URL that granawin.com is the intended target before publishing.

_research_confidence: MEDIUM-HIGH overall — notably stronger than several other entries in this series because the operator's own Terms and Conditions page (playlukkicasino.com/terms-and-conditions) could be read directly and explicitly lists only 12 restricted countries (USA, UK, China, North Korea, Russia, Iran, Sudan, Syria, Cuba, Belarus, Central African Republic, Libya) — none of Ireland, Finland, Brazil, South Africa, or Canada appear on it, giving reasonably high confidence that all five are genuinely supported markets (Ontario is excluded separately, on AGCO/iGaming Ontario licensing grounds, not the general restricted list). This directly overrides a much longer, clearly stale/templated restricted-country list found on one secondary reviewer, which was disregarded as unreliable. The core 4-deposit welcome package total (€/CA$4,000 + 300 Free Spins) and 40x wagering are well corroborated; the exact free-spin count on the second deposit has a minor unresolved conflict (50 vs 100) noted above. Country-specific currency caps for Brazil and South Africa were not independently confirmed beyond the general EUR/CAD structure, despite both markets showing strong indirect support (local payment methods, supported currencies). The United States is confirmed excluded, both via the operator's own T&Cs and multiple secondary sources. Germany intentionally omitted per project scope.
_brand_disambiguation_notes: Lukki Casino (launched 2024) is confirmed as a genuine Just Entertainment B.V. brand — this operator name and Curaçao licence number are consistent across five or more independent sources, including a direct fetch of the operator's own Terms and Conditions page (playlukkicasino.com/terms-and-conditions). One source (correctcasinos.com) instead names the operator as 'Dama N.V.' — this conflicts with the majority and with the primary-source T&Cs; Dama N.V. is actually the operator behind a different, unrelated sister-style casino (LuckyDreams, covered elsewhere in this research series), so that single claim looks like a mix-up rather than fact and was not used. A player complaint on one review site also referenced 'Dama N.V. rogue behaviour' in connection with Lukki, likely the same confusion. Note also that lukki.com itself blocked direct automated access during this research (bot detection); all data below comes from the operator's own Terms and Conditions page (reachable via search) plus independent reviewers, not a direct homepage fetch.

_brand_disambiguation_notes: CRITICAL, UNRESOLVED CONFLICT. The supplied URL (evospin10.com) is confirmed, via direct fetch, to be AI-generated/templated spam content — not the operator's real site — that itself points to 'Evospin.com' as the genuine domain within its own filler text. Independent reviewers of 'Evospin Casino' at evospin.com then split into three incompatible operator/licence claims: (1) N1 Interactive Ltd under a Malta Gaming Authority licence — the most-repeated claim, corroborated across six or more sources spanning 2022 through 2026, including what reads as a genuine Trustpilot player complaint invoking UK Gambling Commission guidance; (2) Bets Entertainment N.V. under a Curaçao GCB licence — from one detailed, recent (April 2026), Canada-focused reviewer; (3) Zosma LLC under an Anjouan licence, #ALSI-132405050-FI3 — this exact licence number was already flagged as an unreliable, likely-reused/templated fake in a prior casino's research in this same project, so this third claim was disregarded entirely. Between the remaining two, no clear tiebreaker was found — it's plausible the casino changed operating entity/licence at some point and sources are simply out of sync with each other, but this could not be confirmed. Welcome bonus headline figures also conflict directly between two otherwise-detailed sources (125% up to €2,500 + 125 FS vs. up to $2,000 + 100 FS). Given this compounding uncertainty, no bonus figures are presented with confidence below — this entry should not be published until the operator identity is manually confirmed directly against the live evospin.com site.

license: Kahnawake Gaming Commission
Alcohol and Gaming Commission of Ontario (AGCO) — separate Ontario-specific registration
eCOGRA certified
operator: Apollo Entertainment Ltd (Malta), part of the Casino Rewards Group
_brand_disambiguation_notes: Zodiac Casino at zodiac.casino is confirmed as the genuine, long-established casino (a well-known member of the Casino Rewards Group, famous for its '80 chances to win $1 million for $1' promotion) — fetched directly, confirming the operator (Apollo Entertainment Ltd, Malta), Kahnawake licensing, and the current welcome package terms directly from the operator's own current homepage. Notably, and unlike almost every other casino researched in this project, this operator IS separately licensed for Ontario by the AGCO (confirmed by an explicit 'Ontario' jurisdiction listing and an Ontario-specific 19+ age requirement, versus 18+ elsewhere) — however, the specific welcome bonus researched here is explicitly confirmed by a dedicated Canadian reviewer as NOT available to players physically in Ontario, who are instead served by a separate, AGCO-compliant version of the site with its own terms not covered by this research. A significant, specific restricted-country list from one detailed source explicitly names both Ireland and South Africa as currently blocked, and a second source's country list (though ambiguously framed) closely mirrors that same restricted set for several European countries including Finland — this directly conflicts with generic multi-casino comparison-site navigation lists (chipy.com, casinosanalyzer.com) that include Ireland, Finland, South Africa, and Brazil as if they were confirmed-available markets; those generic lists were treated as unreliable site-wide navigation rather than casino-specific confirmation, consistent with a pattern seen elsewhere in this research project.
_research_confidence: MEDIUM-HIGH overall, with the unusual strength of having fetched the operator's own current homepage directly, confirming operator identity, licensing, and the full welcome package structure and total with HIGH confidence. Canada is the clear, dominant, extremely well-documented market for this casino (recent winners lists, dedicated reviewers, and the operator's own site all skew heavily Canadian) — and it is one of very few casinos in this research series confirmed to hold a genuine AGCO/Ontario licence, though the specific welcome bonus researched here is explicitly excluded for Ontario players, who are served by a separate registration not covered here. Ireland and South Africa were deliberately excluded, based on an explicit, detailed restricted-country list from one reviewer plus a closely-matching pattern in a second source — this took priority over generic multi-casino comparison-site country lists that listed both as 'available,' which were judged unreliable (a recurring pattern in this project). Finland was also excluded on the same reasoning, given its consistent appearance alongside Ireland in the apparent restricted-country pattern, though this is less directly confirmed than Ireland or South Africa specifically. Brazil was omitted — it did not appear on the explicit restricted list, but no source positively confirmed it as an accepted market either, so per the 'do not guess' rule it was left out. The United States is implicitly excluded via the extensive US-state-by-state restricted list found. Germany intentionally omitted per project scope (and is, in any case, also named as restricted by the same detailed source).

_brand_disambiguation_notes: CRITICAL, UNRESOLVED CONFLICT. Independent reviewers of vincispin.com (launched 2024) give at least three different operator names — Terdersoft B.V., Roosobash SRL (a Costa Rica-registered company, with one reviewer explicitly noting Costa Rica doesn't require a gambling licence to operate, a materially different regulatory picture than a Curaçao licence), and Betmen Affiliates — and two different Curaçao licence numbers, while one detailed reviewer (Casino.Guru) states plainly that no licence has been granted by any regulator at all. Separately, and more concerning: one reviewer specifically notes that VinciSpin's Canadian-facing version of the site does not display licensing information in its footer, while the European version does — this is a specific, notable red flag rather than a generic oversight, and is paired with several player complaints on Casino.Guru describing delayed or missing withdrawals and accounts closed without explanation. Welcome bonus headline figures also conflict substantially across sources (€500 + 350 FS vs. €1,000 + 200 FS vs. $3,000 + 350 spins for what should be the same offer), too much to reconcile into one confident figure. Given the compounding uncertainty around operator identity, licensing status, and the specific Canada-licensing red flag, no bonus data is presented with confidence below, and this entry should not be published without a fresh, direct check of the live site's own footer and terms.
_research_confidence: VERY LOW. This entry could not be responsibly completed. Operator identity and even basic licensing status are disputed across otherwise reasonable-looking sources, and one reviewer flagged a specific, concrete red flag about missing licence disclosure specifically on the Canadian version of the site — which would ordinarily have been the strongest candidate market here given confirmed Interac/CAD payment support. Given that specific flag, Canada was not included despite the payment-method signal. No country was included in the bonus's countryRelated list, since no market could be confirmed as supported with a level of confidence appropriate given the surrounding uncertainty about who even operates this site. Strongly recommend a human directly check vincispin.com's own footer and terms (and specifically compare what displays for a Canadian IP vs. a European one) before this entry is used for anything.

_brand_disambiguation_notes: IMPORTANT: the supplied URL, caspero.net, is not the real casino — it's a static template site built with Hugo (a static-site generator visible in the page metadata), a technology real gambling platforms don't run on since they require live account systems and licensed RNG integration. Every interactive element loops back to other pages on the same static site rather than an actual gaming platform, and no verifiable license number appears anywhere on it. The genuine, actively-reviewed Caspero Casino (launched 2025) is owned by NovaForge Ltd — the same operator behind Gizbo Casino and Flagman Casino, both researched earlier in this project — under an Anjouan Gaming Authority licence, consistent across six or more independent reviewers, several with detailed, Canada-specific content (Interac support, specific T&Cs). One source instead claims a UK Gambling Commission licence for a 'Caspero Casino UK' — this conflicts sharply with the clear majority and likely describes either stale/unreliable content or an unrelated entity; it was not used. This casino also runs on a shared platform (referred to by one reviewer as 'IG Play') alongside several other operator names (Estolio Limited, Liernin Enterprises Ltd, Rabidi N.V., Stellar Ltd) tied to the same group — expected for this kind of white-label setup, not a sign of confusion. The exact canonical domain for the real casino could not be independently confirmed from available sources (a Canada-specific domain, casperocasino.co, appeared in one source but wasn't verified as primary) — recommend confirming this before publishing.
_research_confidence: LOW-MEDIUM overall, and this entry required a significant correction at the outset since the supplied URL wasn't the casino at all. Once that was set aside, operator identity (NovaForge Ltd, Anjouan Gaming Authority) is reasonably well corroborated, and the core casino welcome bonus structure is consistent across several sources — one of which (casizon.com) provided an unusually detailed T&C breakdown that lent good confidence to the wagering terms. Canada is the only market with solid, specific documentation (multiple Canada-focused reviewers, confirmed Interac support), so it's the only country included; Ontario exclusion is inferred rather than directly confirmed. Ireland, Finland, Brazil, South Africa, and the United States were all omitted — no source mentioned any of them in connection with this casino. AskGamblers separately notes a 'Very low Safety Index' rating and describes a player complaint involving delayed/stopped payouts after a large win — worth flagging as a caution, though it doesn't change the bonus-data findings above. Given the URL mismatch, this entry should not be published without first confirming the real casino's canonical domain. Germany intentionally omitted per project scope.

_brand_disambiguation_notes: IMPORTANT — flag for editorial review before publishing: this casino has a genuinely unresolved licensing/operator identity, not just a numbers discrepancy. Several independent review sites (casino.guru, AboutSlots, Tribuna) explicitly state the operator's own site discloses NO licensing or ownership information in its footer/T&Cs, and casino.guru states 'as far as we know, DivaSpin Casino does not have a gambling license.' Meanwhile a separate cluster of sources (betpokies.org, casinobankingmethods.com, several .com mirror/affiliate domains such as divaspin4.com, divaspin.co.com, divaspin-australia.com) claim it is licensed under 'Anjouan Gaming' and operated by 'NovaForge N.V.' / 'NovaForge LTD'. A third source (divaspincasino.online, a clear mirror/marketing domain) claims Curaçao eGaming, and a template/placeholder page (diva-spin.org) even lists 'Licensed under [Licensed by Curaçao eGaming under license number XYZ]' — literally an unfilled template field — alongside an implausible claim of holding both MGA and UKGC licenses simultaneously, which is inconsistent with everything else and was not used. A Trustpilot reviewer also states outright 'DIVASPIN.COM IS NOT LICENCED.' Given this pattern (unverifiable footer disclosure + a scam-template artifact + conflicting third-party claims + an unresolved player payout dispute on Trustpilot), operator and license have been left blank rather than guessed, and this casino should go to broader editorial review before any bonus content is published, consistent with how other ambiguous-legitimacy casinos in this batch have been handled. Numerous near-identical mirror/marketing domains exist (divaspin4.com, divaspin.co.com, divaspin.co, divaspin.ie, diva-spin.org, diva-spin.casino, divaspincasino1.com, divaspincasino.online, divaspin-australia.com) with varying currency defaults (AUD-focused pages quote different totals, e.g. up to A$4,500) — these were treated as secondary/regional mirrors of the same brand rather than distinct operators, but their AUD-specific per-deposit-tier breakdowns were NOT used since they aren't confirmed to apply to the EUR/international terms below.
_research_confidence: MEDIUM for bonus terms, LOW for operator/license identity — this casino needs editorial escalation before publishing, not just a standard fact-check. The core welcome package (250%/€3,000/350 FS/Bonus Crab), weekend reload, weekday spins, and both cashback offers are corroborated across the operator's own site and 2-3 independent reviewers each, giving reasonable confidence in the bonus mechanics themselves. However, the operator's identity and licensing status could not be resolved: reputable review aggregators (casino.guru, AboutSlots, Tribuna) state plainly that the official site discloses no license or ownership information, while a cluster of SEO/affiliate mirror domains claim an Anjouan Gaming license under 'NovaForge N.V.'/'NovaForge LTD' — a claim that could not be verified against the operator's own site. Given this, and an unresolved player payout dispute visible on Trustpilot, this entry should not go to publication without a manual legitimacy review, consistent with how other casinos of uncertain standing have been handled in this batch. IE, FI, CA (excluding Ontario), ZA and BR were all confirmed supported via the operator's own country selector (per correctcasinos.com's capture of it) plus country-specific bonus-term details found independently (BRL min deposit and 10x cashout cap for BR, explicit Ontario exclusion for CA, Interac/CAD for CA). US is explicitly excluded. CA-ON was not included as a separate entry since the operator's own bonus terms state it is not available in Ontario.

Higher tiers get faster withdrawals, higher limits, personal manager, and exclusive bonuses. PRO level gets no-wager bonuses and 5% live casino cashback with zero wagering. Daily withdrawal limits: START €200, MID €4,000, TOP €10,000, PRO €15,000+.

_brand_disambiguation_notes: Primary domain starda.casino (not a mirror). Sister brand to Monro Casino and 1Go Casino (same operator Galaktika N.V.) but distinct casino with separate branding, bonus structure, and larger game portfolio. Do not confuse with unrelated 'Starda' brands. Uses red/white star branding. Regional domains include starda.casino/fr, /es, /ru, /de. Belarus explicitly restricted per NoDeposit365.
_research_confidence: MEDIUM — Brand identity, operator (Galaktika N.V.), Curaçao license OGL/2024/169/0146, and launch year confirmed. GEO acceptance inferred from regional domains, affiliate targeting, currency support (C$, NZ$, A$, R$, CLP, MXN implied), and restricted country patterns (Belarus, UK, US, France, Netherlands, Spain, Italy, South Africa, Tunisia, Slovenia, Poland explicitly blocked or restricted per various sources). However, bonus amounts have MAJOR conflicts: welcome bonus reported as 100% + 500 FS (Canada/general), 50% up to €300 (Ireland/Finland), 300% across 4 deposits (some affiliates), up to $2,000 + 500 FS (Stardabet). Wagering varies 30x(D+B) to 45xB. No-deposit codes vary (BEST777 expired May 2026, EXCLUSIVE, TRIBUNAFS, NDP). Live verification of current offers strongly recommended before publishing.
